---
title: "Wulfing NR913 Final Project"
author: "Sophie Wulfing"
date: '2022-04-04'
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

#open session 11, 12, 14
#FinalProj and ModelTests

library(R2jags)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(multcompView)
library(unikn)

setwd("~/UNH_Docs/NR913")

cephs <- read.csv("C:/Users/sophi/Documents/UNH_Docs/Proposal/CNH2_data/cleanData.csv")

site_data <- cephs %>%
    group_by(REGION) %>%
    summarise(Count = n()) %>%
    filter(Count > 150)

ceph_sites <- cephs %>%
  filter(REGION %in% site_data$REGION)

ceph_sites2 <- transform(ceph_sites,id=as.numeric(factor(REGION)))

ceph_model <- transform(cephs,id=as.numeric(factor(REGION)), sex_id=(as.numeric(factor(FISHER_SEX))-1))
ceph_sex <- ceph_model %>% drop_na(sex_id)

effort <- ceph_model %>%
  filter(!is.na(FISHING_TIME) & !is.na(PRICE_USD) & !is.na(FISHER_SEX) & as.numeric(FISHING_TIME_ADJ) != 0)
effort$TIME_VAL <- effort$PRICE_USD/as.numeric(effort$FISHING_TIME_ADJ)

purps <- "#440154FF"
midnight <- "#414487FF"
dkteal <- "#2A788EFF"
limabean <- "#7AD151FF"
bananas <- "#FDE725FF"


```

```{r sitewt, echo = TRUE}

#Compare your two DIC's 
#Do I need a pval?


#Visualize Data-FIX THIS
boxplot(as.numeric(AVG_WT) ~ REGION,
        ylab = "Average wt of Cepalopod",
        xlab = "Site",
        # outline = TRUE,
        # notch = FALSE,
        # staplewex = 0.2,
        # boxwex = 0.4,
        # border = TRUE,
        data = ceph_sites2)

#sink("cephs_sitewt.txt")
cat(" 
    model {
    
    # PRIORS
    for(i in 1:nsites){
     alpha[i] ~ dnorm(mu_site,tau_site)
    }
    
    mu_site ~ dnorm(0,0.001)
    tau_site <- 1/sigma_site^2
    sigma_site ~ dunif(0,100)
    
    for(i in 1:nsites){
    tau[i] <- 1/sigma[i]^2
    sigma[i] ~ dunif(0,100)
    }
    
    # LIKELIHOOD
    for(i in 1:n){
    mass[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i],tau[site[i]])
    mu[i] <- alpha[site[i]] #Mean will vary by site. 3 alphas, 1 per site. loop through every row of your dataset.
    } # i
    
     # DERIVED QUANTITIES
    for(i in 1:nsites){
    avg[i] <- (alpha[i]*stdev_data) + mean_data
    stan_dev[i] <- (sigma[i]*stdev_data) 
    }

    mu_site_m <- (mu_site*stdev_data) + mean_data
    sigma_site_m <- (sigma_site*stdev_data) 
    
    # generate new data from the fitted model
    for(i in 1:n){
    y.new[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i],tau[i])   #Generates new data for each run of mcmc
     }
    #tukey's HSD,  max liklihood, bonferroni
    #not doing this will give you artifically high p vals, the things above will be alittle more accurate
    #https://www.r-bloggers.com/2021/08/how-to-perform-tukey-hsd-test-in-r/
    } # end of model  
    ",fill = TRUE)
#sink()

win.data <- list(mass   = log(as.numeric(ceph_sites2$AVG_WT)),
                 site   = ceph_sites2$id, 
                 n      = nrow(ceph_sites2),
                 nsites = length(unique(ceph_sites2$id)),
                 stdev_data = sd(as.numeric(ceph_sites2$AVG_WT), na.rm = TRUE),
                 mean_data = mean(as.numeric(ceph_sites2$AVG_WT), na.rm = TRUE))

# Initial values
inits <- function()list(alpha = rnorm(7), sigma = rep(rlnorm(1),7))

# Parameters monitored
params <- c("alpha",
            "sigma",
            "avg",
            "stan_dev",
            "mu_site_m",
            "sigma_site_m",
            #"Bayes.P"
            "y.new"
) 

# MCMC settings
ni <- 1000; nt <- 3; nb <- 100; nc <- 3


out_stwt <- jags(win.data, inits, params, "cephs_sitewt.txt", 
            n.chains = nc, n.thin = nt, n.iter = ni, n.burnin = nb, 
            working.directory = getwd())
#print(out_stwt, dig = 1) 

print(exp(out_stwt$BUGSoutput$sims.list$alpha[1,]))

par(mfrow = c(3,3),
    mar = c(5.1, 4.1, 3.1, 0.5)) #b, l ,t ,r
boxplot(as.numeric(AVG_WT) ~ REGION, 
        ylab = "Wt",
        xlab = "Site",
        staplewex = 0.2,
        boxwex = 0.4,
        frame.plot = FALSE,
        col = c(purps,limabean,bananas),
        data = ceph_sites2,
        main = "Actual data")

for(i in 1:8){
boxplot(exp(out_stwt$BUGSoutput$sims.list$y.new[i,]) ~ ceph_sites2$id, 
        ylab = "Wt",
        xlab = "Site",
        staplewex = 0.2,
        boxwex = 0.4,
        frame.plot = FALSE,
        col = c(purps,limabean,bananas),
        main = paste0("Model-generated data; iter = ",i))
}
dev.off()
```

```{r tukey, echo = FALSE}

#https://r-graph-gallery.com/84-tukey-test.html
#https://www.r-bloggers.com/2021/08/how-to-perform-tukey-hsd-test-in-r/

model <- aov(as.numeric(AVG_WT)~REGION, ceph_sites2)
tukTest <- TukeyHSD(model, conf.level=.95)

plot(TukeyHSD(model, conf.level=.95), las = 2, cex.axis=.5, mar = c(5.1,15,1.4,2.1))

generate_label_df <- function(TUKEY, variable){
 
     # Extract labels and factor levels from Tukey post-hoc 
     Tukey.levels <- TUKEY[["REGION"]][,4]
     Tukey.labels <- data.frame(multcompLetters(Tukey.levels)['Letters'])
     
     #I need to put the labels in the same order as in the boxplot :
     Tukey.labels$treatment=rownames(Tukey.labels)
     Tukey.labels=Tukey.labels[order(Tukey.labels$treatment) , ]
     return(Tukey.labels)
}

LABELS <- generate_label_df(tukTest, "ceph_sites2$REGION")

# A panel of colors to draw each group with the same color :
my_colors <- hcl.colors(5, palette = "YlOrRd", alpha = NULL, fixup = TRUE)

# Draw the basic boxplot
a <- boxplot(as.numeric(ceph_sites2$AVG_WT) ~ ceph_sites2$REGION , ylim= c(0 , 7.5) , ylab="Avg ceph weight (kg)", xlab = "Region", main="", cex.axis = 0.67, col=my_colors[as.numeric(factor(LABELS[,1]))])
 
# I want to write the letter over each box. Over is how high I want to write it.
over <- 0.12*max( a$stats[nrow(a$stats),] )
 
#Add the labels
# text( c(1:nlevels(as.factor(ceph_sites2$REGION))) , a$stats[nrow(a$stats)-1,]+over , LABELS[,1], col=my_colors[as.numeric(factor(LABELS[,1]))] )
mark(
  LABELS[,1],
  x = c((1:nlevels(as.factor(ceph_sites2$REGION)))+.02),
  y = a$stats[nrow(a$stats)-1,]+ over,
  y_layout = "even",
  col = my_colors[as.numeric(factor(LABELS[,1]))],
  col_bg = "white",
  cex = .9,
  font = 1,
  new_plot = "none"
)

```

```{r sexno, echo = FALSE}
#TTEST
#Talk about differences in bayes p val and dic btwn models

poisson.t.test <- glm(ceph_model$NO_CAUGHT ~ ceph_model$sex_id, family = poisson)
summary(poisson.t.test) #effect of arable are significant. ESTIMATES ARE ON LOG SCALE

#Visualize Data-FIX THIS
boxplot(NO_CAUGHT ~ FISHER_SEX, 
        ylab = "No caught per landing",
        xlab = "Site",
        outline = TRUE,
        notch = FALSE,
        staplewex = 0.2,
        boxwex = 0.4,
        border = TRUE,
        data = ceph_model)

sink("cephs_sexno.txt")
cat(" 
    model {
    
    # PRIORS
    beta0 ~ dnorm(0,0.001)  # intercept
    beta1 ~ dnorm(0,0.001)  # effect of M/F, IDK
    tau <- 1/sigma^2
    sigma ~ dunif(0,10)
    
    # LIKELIHOOD
    for(i in 1:n){
    cephs[i] ~ dpois(lambda[i]) #error term built in bc lambda is both mean and var
    log(lambda[i]) <- beta0 + beta1 * sex[i] +eps[i]# sex needs to be 0s and 1s. predicting log of lambda, not lambda
    #lambda[i] <- exp(beta0 + beta1 * sex[i] ) Alternative form
    eps[i] ~ dnorm(0,tau)
    
    # CHECK MODEL FIT
    presid[i] <- (cephs[i] - lambda[i])/sqrt(lambda[i])          # Pearson residual- corrects for larger mean => larger var
    cephs.new[i] ~ dpois(lambda[i])                              # new data from fitted model
    presid.new[i] <- (cephs.new[i] - lambda[i])/sqrt(lambda[i])  # Pearson residual for new data

    D[i] <- presid[i]^2          # squared Pearson residuals for observed data
    D.new[i] <- presid.new[i]^2  # squared Pearson residuals for new data
    
    
    } # i
    

    # CHECK MODEL FIT - BAYESIAN P-VALUE
    D.sum <- sum(D[])                      # sum of squared residuals for raw data
    D.new.sum <- sum(D.new[])              # same, but for model-generated data
    Bayes.P <- step(D.new.sum/D.sum - 1)   # step(x) tests if x >= 0
    
    } # end of model  
    ",fill = TRUE)
sink()

win.data <- list(cephs = as.integer(ceph_sex$NO_CAUGHT), 
                 sex  = ceph_sex$sex_id,
                 n  = nrow(ceph_sex))

# Initial values
inits <- function()list(beta0  = rnorm(1),
                        beta1  = rnorm(1))

# Parameters monitored
params <- c("beta0",
            "beta1",
            "Bayes.P",
            "D.sum",
            "D.new.sum",
            "cephs.new")

# MCMC settings
ni <- 3000; nt <- 1; nb <- 1000; nc <- 3

out_sexno <- jags(win.data, inits, params, "cephs_sexno.txt", 
                  n.chains = nc, n.thin = nt, n.iter = ni, n.burnin = nb, 
                  working.directory = getwd())
print(out_sexno, dig = 2)
hist(as.numeric(out_sexno$BUGSoutput$mean$cephs.new))
# plot parameter posteriors
o <- out_sexno$BUGSoutput$sims.list

d <- density(o$beta0)
d2 <- density(o$beta1)

plot(d,
     xlab = "Posterior distribution",
     ylim = c(0,3),
     xlim = c(-1,1.5),
     main = "",
     las = 1)
polygon(d, col = rgb(182,207,182, alpha = 175, maxColorValue = 255), 
        border = "black", lwd = 2)
polygon(d2, col = rgb(255,255,181, alpha = 175, maxColorValue = 255), 
        border = "black", lwd = 2)

# convert to raw scale and plot - what model thinks means are
d <- density(exp(o$beta0))
d2 <- density(exp(o$beta0 + o$beta1))

plot(d,
     xlab = "Posterior distribution",
     main = "Expected no caught by sex",
     xlim = c(0,9),
     las = 1)
polygon(d, col = rgb(182,207,182, alpha = 175, maxColorValue = 255), 
        border = "black", lwd = 3)
polygon(d2, col = rgb(255,255,181, alpha = 175, maxColorValue = 255), 
        border = "black", lwd = 3)


# plot model generated new data - what model thinks data looks like
par(mfrow = c(2,1))
hist(o$cephs.new[,ceph_sex$FISHER_SEX == "M"],
     xlab = "Cephs per catch",
     col = rgb(182,207,182, alpha = 195, maxColorValue = 255),
     main = NULL,
     xlim = c(0,18),
     breaks = 10)
hist(o$cephs.new[ceph_sex$FISHER_SEX == "F"],
     xlab = "Cephs per catch",
     col = rgb(255,255,181, alpha = 125, maxColorValue = 255),
     main = NULL)#,
     #add = TRUE)

```
